subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, EC/Council, ECB, OECD, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, IMF COFER) + Austrian official (Statistik Austria, OeNB) + German-language press (Der Standard, Die Presse, ORF, WIFO).

Politics: the far-right FPO won a 2024 plurality but was kept from power by a record-long three-party coalition formed in 2025 - fragmented but orderly. Austria is a EUROZONE member and constitutionally neutral (non-NATO).

Standout features: excellent institutions, healthcare, infrastructure, and safety, against a recent fiscal deterioration (an EU deficit procedure) and a far-right political rise. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone on the election/neutrality framing.

economic.notes: An advanced, German-integrated export economy and sound banks, against a recent fiscal deterioration and recession.

fiscal_state.notes: An EU excessive-deficit procedure opened in 2025 (deficit ~4.5-4.7%, debt ~80%), having been the EU's last economy still in recession, with a correction path to under 3% by 2028. (EC; Council EDP)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - credible and anti-inflation but not sovereign. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: A participant in the euro, the world's second reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Was stickily above the euro-area average, now easing toward target. (OeNB; Statistik Austria)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised, with a legacy Russia exposure being wound down under ECB pressure. (ECB; OeNB FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Advanced, diversified industry and exports deeply integrated with Germany, against an energy-cost and Russia-gas-legacy drag, no resource curse. (OECD; WIFO)

institutional.notes: Independent courts, excellent statistics, and high administrative quality, with media-ownership concentration the caveat.

rule_of_law.notes: Independent courts with high WJP scores. (WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: Statistik Austria is credible and ESS-compliant. (Eurostat)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~22nd) but flagged for concentrated ownership and prior governing-party advertising-corruption scandals. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Strong liberties and a cohesive welfare society, against a far-right rise and an ageing population.

political_stability.notes: The far-right FPO won a 2024 plurality but was excluded by a record-long three-party coalition formed in 2025 - fragmented but orderly, with institutions holding. (election data; ORF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful EU routes (a Red-White-Red card) but a restrictive, tightening asylum stance. (EC; EUAA)

social_cohesion.notes: A cohesive welfare society, with immigration/integration polarisation rising. (OECD; Der Standard)

demographics.notes: Ageing, low fertility, and migration-dependent growth. (Statistik Austria; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: EU-anchored and conflict-free, but constitutionally neutral with a limited hard-security commitment.

alliance_reliability.notes: An EU member but constitutionally neutral and non-NATO - a limited hard-security commitment, with defence rising from a low base. (Defense News)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict, with a residual Russia-energy-dependence legacy. (EC; OeNB)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EC)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety, against Alpine flood and glacier-loss exposure.

climate.notes: Temperate, high readiness, but Alpine glacier/snow loss, rockfall, and 2024-type flooding. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6 mountains)

crime_safety.notes: Low crime - a clear strength. (Eurostat; Statistik Austria)

disaster_insurance.notes: A functioning Alpine-hazard market, with a coverage gap exposed by the 2024 floods (~45% insured). (Munich Re; VVO)

summary: A high-income country with excellent institutions, healthcare, infrastructure, and personal safety, and an advanced, German-integrated export economy. The drags are a recent fiscal deterioration (an EU excessive-deficit procedure after the EU's last recession), a sticky inflation legacy, and a far-right political rise - though that party was kept from power by an orderly, if fragmented, three-party coalition. Membership of the euro pools monetary policy at a credible ECB, while constitutional neutrality keeps the hard-security commitment limited.
